name: a_b_adapter_comparison_v1
description: A/B comparison evaluation dataset for deciding between adapter variants. Covers version comparison, architecture comparison (LoRA rank, target modules), inconclusive results handling, mixed-signal triage, and experimental methodology. All examples grounded in adapterOS adapter lifecycle, statistical testing, and deployment decision frameworks.

entries.notes: 2000 examples across five categories: version_comparison (400), architecture_comparison (400), inconclusive_results (400), mixed_results (400), methodology (400). Each example contains a realistic A/B evaluation scenario with statistical reasoning, metric interpretation, and deployment decisions.

provenance.review_notes: All adapter identifiers, metric names, and deployment workflows reflect actual adapterOS registry and orchestrator patterns. Statistical methodology follows standard paired evaluation practice with bootstrap CI and paired t-test. Decision framework aligned with adapterOS production readiness policy gates.

evaluation_gates: Statistical test is appropriate for the comparison type (paired t-test or bootstrap CI)
Sample size is at least 100 for any comparison claiming statistical significance
p-value threshold is 0.05 unless explicitly justified otherwise
Confidence intervals are reported alongside point estimates
Deployment decision follows the decision framework: better->deploy, no-diff->keep, worse->reject, mixed->human
Metric names match adapterOS eval dimensions: accuracy, fluency, relevance, safety, latency_ms, memory_mb
Adapter identifiers use the adapterOS naming convention: name/rNNN
Architecture comparisons include resource cost analysis alongside quality metrics
Inconclusive results explain the null result and recommend next steps
Mixed results identify the tradeoff dimensions and escalation criteria
intent: Teach adapters to rigorously compare two adapter variants using statistical methods, interpret multi-dimensional evaluation results, make sound deployment decisions, and design fair A/B experiments. The dataset emphasizes that 'no significant difference' is a valid and informative outcome, and that mixed results require human judgment rather than mechanical rules.

subcategories.version_comparison: Compare revision r001 vs r002 (or similar) of the same adapter. Clear statistical winner on primary metrics. Includes effect size, confidence interval, and unambiguous deploy/reject decision.
subcategories.architecture_comparison: Compare different LoRA configurations (rank, target modules, alpha) trained on the same data. Tradeoff analysis between quality, inference latency, and memory footprint. Decision depends on deployment constraints.
subcategories.inconclusive_results: No statistically significant difference between adapters. Interpret the null result correctly: underpowered study vs true equivalence. Recommend next steps: increase sample size, refine metrics, or keep current adapter.
subcategories.mixed_results: Adapter A wins on some dimensions, adapter B on others. Multi-criteria decision analysis. When to escalate to human review, how to weight dimensions for a given use case, and how to present the tradeoff clearly.
subcategories.methodology: How to design, execute, and analyze a fair A/B adapter comparison. Controlling confounds, avoiding data contamination, sample size calculation, choosing metrics, and reporting results. Experimental design best practices.
